items.title: CVE-2024-52911 - Script Interpreter Remote Crash
items.desc: After Bitcoin Core 0.14.0 and before Bitcoin Core 29.0, validating a specially-crafted block may cause the node to access previously freed memory. During validation, necessary data required for checking inputs for each transaction is pre-calculated and cached. For specially crafted invalid blocks, it was possible for this data to be destroyed while it was still being accessed by a background validation thread. An attacker capable of mining a block with sufficient proof-of-work could have exploited this to crash victim nodes. Because of the nature of use-after-free bugs, it is possible that the crash could have been used for remote code execution, though constraints on the input (block) data make this unlikely. This issue is considered High severity. Details By default, script validation for new blocks is dispatched to background threads via a vector of CScriptCheck functors. Each CScriptCheck holds a pointer to a PrecomputedTransactionData object which stores some data needed by each input in the transaction. Because it stores a pointer and not the data itself, care must be taken to ensure that the PrecomputedTransactionData outlives the CScriptCheck. The script checks lifetime is enforced by an RAII class, CCheckQueueControl. However, the control is intantiated before the precomputed transaction data. Because local objects in C++ are destructed in reverse order of construction, this means the vector of PrecomputedTransactionData is destroyed before the CCheckQueueControl. This is not an issue when the block is valid, as CCheckQueueControl::Wait() will be called before the function returns and the PrecomputedTransactionData gets destroyed. However, in case of an early return (when a separate check fails) a background script thread may read the precomputed transaction data after it was destroyed. An attacker could exploit this to crash victim nodes at the expense of a valid PoW at tip. Attribution Cory Fields (MIT DCI) discovered this vulnerability and responsibly disclosed it in a detailed report containing a proof of concept for reproduction and a proposed mitigation. Timeline 2024-11-02 Cory Fields privately reports the bug 2024-11-06 Pieter Wuille pushes a covert fix to already open PR #31112 which works around the issue by removing the early returns 2024-12-03 PR #31112 is merged 2025-04-12 Bitcoin Core version 29.0 is released with a fix 2026-04-19 The last vulnerable Bitcoin Core version (28.x) goes end of life 2026-05-05 Public disclosure.

items.title: Michael Saylor's Strategy signals potential bitcoin sale to fund dividends obligations
items.desc: Michael Saylor proposes using bitcoin sales to support dividends, as Strategy reported a $12.54 billion Q1 loss.

items.title: Trust in crypto remains biggest barrier to adoption, say Consensus Miami 2026 panelists
items.desc: Speakers point to complexity, poor user experience and lack of transparency as key obstacles for mainstream users.

items.title: Kelp Blames LayerZero for $292 Million Hack, Plans Switch to Chainlink
items.desc: The protocol shift comes as a $71 million court fight continues to unfold.

items.title: Forward Industries, RockawayX back OnRe to build onchain reinsurance on Solana
items.desc: Investors back a model that moves insurance risk and capital flows onto blockchain rails, as onchain reinsurance looks to attract institutional participation.

items.title: Ripple CEO says market structure bill not a ‘done deal,’ despite stablecoin compromise
items.desc: Brad Garlinghouse addressed attendees at a Tuesday crypto conference on the progress of the CLARITY Act after US lawmakers announced a compromise on stablecoin yield that could advance the legislation.

items.title: The world's entire economy will be tokenized, says Consensys’ Joseph Lubin
items.desc: The Consensys CEO and founder said tokenization can be traced back to Ethereum, the blockchain he helped co-found.

items.title: CME Gearing Up to Launch Bitcoin Volatility Futures Independent From BTC’s Price
items.desc: CME's upcoming futures product will track whether the market thinks the price of Bitcoin is about to swing wildly or stay steady.

items.title: Different voices in product, policy and hiring change crypto outcomes, panelists tell Consensus Miami
items.desc: Senior leaders from Mastercard, the Crypto Council for Innovation and Clerisy said the right people in the right rooms can reshape internal decisions, citing examples from stablecoin-linked cards and financial access to staking-policy framing in Washington.

items.title: CFTC looks to cement non-custodial software developer protections following Phantom no-action letter
items.desc: The CFTC is considering writing rules to further cement its stance on whether software developers should register as brokers with the agency.

items.title: State Street says institutions want improved blockchain security in wake of recent DeFi attacks
items.desc: Angus Fletcher, State Street’s head of digital assets said the young crypto industry needs to find solutions now before trillions in RWAs come on-chain.

items.title: How Solana Exchange Drift Plans to Repay Users After $295 Million Crypto Hack
items.desc: Solana's Drift Protocol says most of the stolen funds linked to North Korean hackers remain traceable—and it has a plan to make victims whole.

items.title: Crypto's 'barbell': speculation and stablecoin payments drive adoption, Tempo's Romero says
items.desc: Stablecoins are quietly powering real-world money flows as other crypto experiments than speculative trading have failed to scale, Romero said at Consensus 2026.

items.title: Citi exec says fragmented crypto systems risk repeating old banking problems
items.desc: Tokenized money efforts face limits as corporate clients demand real-time payments that work seamlessly across banks, Citi’s Ryan Rugg said at Consensus in Miami.

items.title: Kelp DAO ditches LayerZero for Chainlink’s cross-chain infrastructure following $292 million exploit
items.desc: Many onlookers blame LayerZero's default infrastructure setup because the cross-chain bridge that was attacked used a 1-of-1 configuration.

items.title: Overseas demand for U.S. equities is growing, says Robinhood's Johan Kerbrart
items.desc: The trading app's senior vice president said tokenization, 24-hour trading, and regulatory shifts are driving broader investor access beyond domestic markets.

items.title: Crypto ETFs go mainstream as traditional finance locks in
items.desc: As traditional finance deepens its footprint, issuers say ETFs are reshaping access, liquidity, and the global structure of digital asset investing.

items.title: Bitcoin short liquidations push BTC to multi-month highs: Is $90K next?
items.desc: Short liquidations and rising open interest may be signs of the bulls’ plan to push the Bitcoin price closer to $90,000.

items.title: Ledger integrates Hyperliquid perps trading in its hardware wallets via Yield.xyz
items.desc: About 20% of Ledger users will be able to access Hyperliquid's onchain perps markets from their hardware wallets.

items.title: Self-directed investors power bitcoin ETF launch despite Morgan Stanley’s scale
items.desc: Strong early demand highlights how investors are driving crypto adoption, even without support from Morgan Stanley’s vast advisor network.

items.title: Strategy Posts $12.5 Billion Q1 Loss as Bitcoin Slump Hammers Massive Holdings
items.desc: Michael Saylor’s Strategy continues to bet big on Bitcoin even as falling prices deliver a punishing quarter for the treasury giant.

items.title: Coinbase Taps Centrifuge as Preferred Tokenization Partner
items.desc: The exchange's strategic investment cements Centrifuge as the go-to issuance layer for compliant onchain assets, starting with a tokenized S&P 500 product for non-U.S. users.

items.title: CME Group to launch cash-settled bitcoin volatility futures
items.desc: Traders use volatility products to speculate on or hedge against swings without having to take a directional bet on prices.

items.title: ‘We do not see a middle ground’: TD Cowen says stablecoin yield fight could still delay crypto bill
items.desc: The dispute could still delay the crypto bill and reduce the chances of it passing this year, despite a proposed compromise, the firm said.

items.title: Kelp DAO Accuses LayerZero of Deflecting Blame for $300M Bridge Hack
items.desc: The liquid restaking protocol argues that the 1-of-1 verifier setup at the center of the April 18 exploit was LayerZero's own documented default.

items.title: Kelp claims that LayerZero approved the setup it blamed for $292 million bridge hack
items.desc: The $292M exploit, linked to North Korean hackers, led Kelp to migrate its rsETH off LayerZero's "OFT" standard to Chainlink's "CCIP."

items.title: Strategy posts $12.54 billion Q1 loss on declining bitcoin price
items.desc: The price of bitcoin fell from about $87,000 to $68,000 during the first three months of 2026.

items.title: Lighter Names USDC as Preferred Stablecoin in New Circle Partnership
items.desc: The agreement spans spot and perpetual trading, settlement, liquidations, and onboarding flows on the decentralized exchange.

items.title: CME Group to Launch Bitcoin Volatility Futures on June 1
items.desc: The derivatives giant is rolling out CFTC-regulated contracts that let traders isolate Bitcoin volatility from price direction, settling to a new 30-day implied volatility benchmark.

items.title: Bitcoin Reclaims $81,000 As ETF Inflows Surge Despite Iran Escalation
items.desc: BTC prints its highest level since January, even as UAE air defenses engage Iranian missiles and drones.

items.title: SoFi to launch its stablecoin on Solana, citing speed and cost
items.desc: SoFiUSD is a fully reserved U.S. dollar stablecoin issued by SoFi Bank, a nationally chartered bank.

items.title: Tennessee Bankers Association names Stablecore as preferred digital asset provider
items.desc: Regional lenders gain access to infrastructure for stablecoins, tokenized deposits and crypto-backed lending without building systems in-house, signaling wider bank adoption.

items.title: Drift outlines a recovery plan for users after $295 million DPRK-linked exploit
items.desc: The lending protocol proposed tokenized claims, a revenue-backed pool and a security overhaul as it works with law enforcement to recover the stolen funds.

items.title: Tokenization won't disrupt banking rails but improve them, Wall Street executives say
items.desc: Executives from Citigroup, JPMorgan and DTCC said at Consensus that genuine client demand is driving real-world use of tokenized assets.

items.title: Bitcoin extends gains to $81,500 as tokenization push lifts Bullish, Galaxy, Centrifuge
items.desc: Crypto market momentum could extend further unless geopolitical risks flare up again, one analyst said.

items.title: a16z Crypto Raises $2.2B Fund to Turn New Infra into Everyday Products
items.desc: a16z crypto's Crypto Fund 5 was announced just a day after Haun Ventures revealed its $1 billion fund focused on crypto and the agentic economy.

items.title: Crypto Fear and Greed Index turns neutral for first time since January: Is $100K BTC next?
items.desc: Improving investor confidence supports Bitcoin’s hold on $80,000, as the Crypto Fear and Greed Index exited the “Extreme Fear” zone and now reads “neutral.” Are the bulls back in control?

items.title: US prosecutors ask for leniency for ex-Celsius exec, citing cooperation
items.desc: While Roni Cohen-Pavon’s lawyers have asked for time served, US Attorney Jay Clayton was unspecific in the federal government’s recommendation, requesting the judge defer to sentencing guidelines.

items.title: French Chipmaker Sequans Dumps Half Its Bitcoin as Treasury Hype Meets Reality
items.desc: Sequans Communications liquidated nearly half its Bitcoin treasury as mounting losses and debt obligations forced a strategic reversal.

items.title: Toncoin Surges as Telegram Takes Direct Control of TON Network
items.desc: Pavel Durov says Telegram will replace the TON Foundation and become the network's largest validator as part of a ‘Make TON Great Again’ roadmap.

items.title: Rep. Steven Horsford pitches PARITY Act as 'durable floor' for crypto tax at Consensus Miami
items.desc: The Nevada Democrat told Professor Yesha Yadav that incremental, bipartisan reform is the path forward as Senate negotiations on the CLARITY Act stall.

items.title: State Street and Galaxy launch fund on Solana designed to ‘sweep’ stablecoins into productive vehicle
items.desc: The State Street Galaxy Onchain Liquidity Sweep Fund lets investors push stablecoins into a yield-bearing tokenized fund.

items.title: Arbitrum DAO Elects Six New Security Council Members Amid Ongoing Kelp Fallout
items.desc: The Security Council has been in the spotlight in recent weeks after its decision to freeze over 30K ETH connected to the Kelp bridge exploit.

items.title: Iggy Azalea Faces Lawsuit Over Solana Meme Coin
items.desc: Investors allege the rapper misled buyers about the token’s real-world utility.

items.title: Solana’s 'Alpenglow' upgrade could arrive next quarter, co-founder Yakovenko says
items.desc: Speaking at Consensus Miami 2026, the Solana co-founder said that he is excited to see 'Alpenglow' upgrade go live later this year, which could come as early as next quarter.

items.title: Western Union’s Solana-based stablecoin could reshape its payment model, analyst says
items.desc: Western Union launched USDPT, a dollar-backed stablecoin on Solana, issued by Anchorage Digital Bank, for 24/7 settlement with agents and partners.

items.title: MoonPay acquires Solana execution layer firm DFlow used by Coinbase, Phantom
items.desc: DFlow is used by both Coinbase and Phantom and has processed over $50 billion in cumulative trading volume.

items.title: Figure targets Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac in mortgage push, citing massive cost cuts for borrowers
items.desc: The firm offers rapid service, targeting the sub-$300,000 loan segment, with HELOC applications approved in 5 minutes and funding in 3 days.

items.title: Bernstein cites $4T tokenized credit opportunity for Figure Technology stock
items.desc: Bernstein points to Figure’s expansion beyond home equity lending into blockchain-based credit, as tokenization begins to scale across broader loan markets.

items.title: 'Big Short' Investor Michael Burry Exits GameStop After eBay Bid
items.desc: GameStop's $55.5 billion eBay bid broke the Berkshire-style thesis the “Big Short” investor had bet on since January.

items.title: XRP price copies 2025 chart fractal that last time sparked 66% gains
items.desc: XRP price broke out of a multi-month bull flag, while other chart technicals are also supporting more upside in the near term.

items.title: Crypto Whale Sues Coinbase Alleging Exchange Refuses to Return Stolen Funds
items.desc: The lawsuit against Coinbase appears to be filed by the victim of a $55 million stablecoin phishing attack pulled off in 2024.

items.title: Securitize Taps Jump and Jupiter to Launch Regulated Trading for Tokenized Equities
items.desc: The three-way partnership pairs Securitize's broker-dealer rails with Jump's PropAMM liquidity engine and Jupiter's Solana-wide aggregation layer.

items.title: Bullish Shares Pop on $4.2 Billion Deal to Acquire Transfer Agent Equiniti
items.desc: NYSE-listed crypto exchange operator Bullish will merge blockchain infrastructure with a transfer agent serving traditional equity markets.

items.title: Ripple CEO Brad Garlinghouse warns next two weeks are critical for crypto legislation
items.desc: The next two weeks will be pivotal in passing broader cryptocurrency legislation, says Ripple CEO Brad Garlinghouse.

items.title: Drift Sets Out Token-Based Recovery Framework for $295M April Exploit
items.desc: The Solana-based perpetuals exchange will issue burn-on-redeem recovery tokens funded by exchange revenue, a $127.5M Tether commitment, and another $20M from partners.

items.title: Ripple CEO Brad Garlinghouse says Clarity better than chaos as Senate hits key moment
items.desc: The crypto market structure Clarity Act needs to get to its next step, a Senate hearing, this month to have a reasonable chance, Garlinghouse said.

items.title: MoneyGram supports crypto-to-cash withdrawals for Kraken users
items.desc: The firms plan to expand this partnership beyond cash withdrawals to support local bank deposits and cross-border remittance flows.

items.title: Wall Street warns human-built markets can’t keep up with machine-speed trading
items.desc: Legacy financial systems face pressure as tokenization and real-time trading push markets toward always-on, automated infrastructure, panelists at Consensus in Miami said on Tuesday.

items.title: Crypto's value is from being outside regulatory apparatus, says Arthur Hayes
items.desc: All that matters for bitcoin’s value proposition is fiat liquidity, said Arthur Hayes, CIO of Maelstrom, at Consensus Miami 2026.

items.title: Sequans Sells Half Its Bitcoin Holdings as Revenue Falls and Losses Mount
items.desc: Bitcoin Magazine Sequans Sells Half Its Bitcoin Holdings as Revenue Falls and Losses Mount Sequans Communications sold 1,025 bitcoin during the first quarter of 2026, cutting its digital asset reserves nearly in half. This post Sequans Sells Half Its Bitcoin Holdings as Revenue Falls and Losses Mount first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.

items.title: Bitcoin ETFs Draw Investment Surge After Brief Lull Threatens Winning Streak
items.desc: Bitcoin funds are flying high again after a short-lived stretch of outflows nearly wrecked their recent run of green weeks.

items.desc: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, NFTs, Web3 and crypto regulation.

items.title: Kaiko flags possible front-running before Robinhood token listings
items.desc: Kaiko said open interest, funding rates and wallet activity showed repeated pre-announcement positioning before several Robinhood token listings.

items.title: Wormhole bridges ‘canonical’ version of Bittensor’s TAO token to Solana
items.desc: The activation is powered by Wormhole Labs’ Sunrise platform, which acts as a "day-one asset gateway" for bridging native assets.

items.title: Coinbase doubles down on Centrifuge investment, taps platform as tokenization partner for Base
items.desc: Centrifuge will be the go-to platform for launching tokenized assets on Base, the Ethereum blockchain incubated by Coinbase.

items.title: Stellar Gets Its First Regulated, Yield-Bearing Stablecoin with YLDS Launch
items.desc: YLDS is an SEC-registered, USD-pegged stable asset from Figure.

items.title: Kraken Partners With MoneyGram to Enable Crypto Cash-Outs at 500,000 Locations Worldwide
items.desc: Bitcoin Magazine Kraken Partners With MoneyGram to Enable Crypto Cash-Outs at 500,000 Locations Worldwide Kraken will let users convert crypto into cash at MoneyGram locations across more than 100 countries, expanding access between digital assets and physical cash networks. This post Kraken Partners With MoneyGram to Enable Crypto Cash-Outs at 500,000 Locations Worldwide first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.

items.title: Andreessen Horowitz Raises $2.2 Billion to Fund Next Wave of Crypto Startups
items.desc: Andreessen Horowitz's latest fund signals institutional confidence in crypto as the investment landscape shows signs of recovery.

items.title: Space and Time rolls out virtual vaults for institutional onchain lending
items.desc: The data blockchain securing onchain finance projects looks to provide lenders and borrowers with agreement-specific collateral solution.

items.title: Binance changes weekend pricing for commodity TradFi futures
items.desc: Binance will shift to an orderbook-weighted pricing model for commodity perpetual futures during off-hours, potentially changing margin and liquidation behavior.

items.title: Bitcoin ‘supercycle’ or bear-market rally? BTC breaking $81K has traders at odds
items.desc: Bitcoin bottom calls grow as analysts target $180,000–$250,000 within a year, fueling debate over whether BTC has resumed its broader bull cycle.

items.title: Crypto products post 5th straight week of inflows despite mid-week selloff
items.desc: Crypto ETPs shed $619 million over four days before a $737 million Friday session rescued the week, extending the inflow streak to $4.02 billion across five weeks.

items.title: Europe should weigh tokenized SEPA payments, Bank of Italy official says
items.desc: The Bank of Italy’s deputy governor floated the evaluation of tokenized SEPA payments, as the ECB experiments with tokenized digital payment frameworks to avoid stablecoin competition.

items.title: Bullish to buy transfer agent Equiniti for $4.2B in tokenization push
items.desc: Bullish has agreed to acquire transfer agent Equiniti as more Wall Street participants accelerate their tokenization initiatives to offer 24/7 trading.

items.title: Bitcoin's $81K Rally Comes Amid 66-Day Negative Funding Streak: Here’s Why
items.desc: Shorts have paid a 12% annualized carry for 66 days amid Bitcoin's climb—analysts say the driver is institutional hedging, not fear.

items.title: Global crypto ETP inflow streak extends to five weeks, masking sharp intraweek reversal: CoinShares
items.desc: The inflows mark the fifth straight positive week, though the total masked strong outflows that were reversed by a single session on Friday.

items.title: Morning Minute: Crypto VC Haun Raises $1B Despite Market Slump
items.desc: Bitcoin is firmly over $80k. The DTCC is about to tokenize everything. And World Liberty Fi countersues Justin Sun.

items.title: Crypto Exchange Bullish Strikes $4.2 Billion Deal for Equiniti to Build Tokenized Securities Infrastructure
items.desc: Bitcoin Magazine Crypto Exchange Bullish Strikes $4.2 Billion Deal for Equiniti to Build Tokenized Securities Infrastructure Bullish has agreed to acquire global transfer agent Equiniti in a $4.2 billion transaction. This post Crypto Exchange Bullish Strikes $4.2 Billion Deal for Equiniti to Build Tokenized Securities Infrastructure first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.

items.title: Ripple to Share North Korean Threat Intelligence With Crypto Industry
items.desc: The move follows two nine-figure exploits of DeFi protocols Drift and KelpDAO in April, as DPRK hackers shift to social engineering tactics.

items.title: UAE free zone launches blockchain-based business IDs for registered firms
items.desc: Innovation City launches a blockchain-based business ID system, giving more than 1,000 firms verifiable onchain credentials for identity and access.

items.title: Telegram to become TON’s largest validator, Durov says
items.desc: Toncoin rose 33.8% after Pavel Durov said Telegram would deepen its role in TON, while details on the foundation and validator structure remain unclear.

items.title: Bitcoin ETFs pull in $532M as BTC reclaims $80K amid ‘post-ceasefire recovery’
items.desc: Spot Bitcoin ETFs pulled in over $532 million on Monday as BTC surged past $80,000 amid improved risk sentiment following the US-Iran ceasefire.

items.title: Stablecoin proposal still ‘falls short’ of protecting bank deposits: US banks
items.desc: US Senator Thom Tillis said the current text of the CLARITY Act offers a compromise for the crypto industry and banks and provides a bipartisan path for the bill’s passage.

items.title: MoonPay buys DFlow to expand into Solana trading infrastructure
items.desc: The deal adds a Solana-based trading infrastructure platform that has processed more than $50 billion in volume and aggregates liquidity across multiple venues.

items.title: Kraken partners with MoneyGram for global crypto cash-outs
items.desc: The partnership enabling conversion of digital assets into cash through MoneyGram’s global network follows a similar move by rival Western Union.

items.title: Polygon rolls out private stablecoin payments targeting institutions
items.desc: The new wallet feature hides senders, receivers and amounts onchain while maintaining compliance through know your transaction screening and auditable files.

items.title: Aave files emergency motion to lift restraining notice on frozen ETH
items.desc: Aave argued that a thief doesn’t gain lawful ownership of property by stealing it and that Gerstein Harrow’s legal argument “defies logic, common sense and the law.”

items.title: Aave Fights to Unfreeze $71 Million as Kelp DAO Hack Spills Into Court
items.desc: A federal case over frozen crypto could determine whether DeFi recovery funds can be seized to satisfy unrelated judgments.

items.title: Polygon Launches Shielded USDC and USDT Payments
items.desc: The Polygon wallet now offers a "Privately Send" option that hides sender, receiver, and amount onchain, with KYT screening built into every transfer.

items.title: Bitcoin-Funded ‘Satoshi Scholarship’ Opens Lomond School Doors to Global Students
items.desc: Bitcoin Magazine Bitcoin-Funded ‘Satoshi Scholarship’ Opens Lomond School Doors to Global Students Lomond School deepens its Bitcoin integration with a fully funded “Satoshi Scholarship,” turning its campus into a live experiment in Bitcoin-powered education. This post Bitcoin-Funded ‘Satoshi Scholarship’ Opens Lomond School Doors to Global Students first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.

items.title: Glamsterdam Upgrade Set To Triple Ethereum's Execution Capacity
items.desc: ePBS, Block-Level Access Lists, and EIP-8037 repricings combine to unlock a 200M gas limit floor, a throughput jump that could keep network fees pinned for years.

items.title: World Liberty Financial Sues Justin Sun for Defamation
items.desc: The move is the latest in an escalating feud and comes just weeks after Sun filed a lawsuit against World Liberty Financial for freezing his WLFI, alongside other allegations.

items.title: Strive’s (ASST) Bitcoin Treasury Crosses 15,000 BTC After $33.9 Million Purchase
items.desc: Bitcoin Magazine Strive’s (ASST) Bitcoin Treasury Crosses 15,000 BTC After $33.9 Million Purchase Strive, Inc. (Nasdaq: ASST) said Monday its Bitcoin treasury has topped 15,000 BTC after it acquired 444 bitcoin for $33.9 million at an average price of $76,307 per coin. This post Strive’s (ASST) Bitcoin Treasury Crosses 15,000 BTC After $33.9 Million Purchase first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.

items.title: DTCC Sets July Launch Window for Tokenized Securities Pilot
items.desc: The DTC unit will begin processing limited tokenized trades in July before opening the service more broadly in October.

items.title: Aave Asks Court to Vacate Restraining Notice Targeting Recovered Kelp DAO Assets
items.desc: The emergency motion challenges a New York court order redirecting recovered Kelp DAO exploit funds toward decades-old terrorism judgments against North Korea.

items.title: Lawyer Attempts to Seize Frozen ETH Linked to Kelp Exploit From Arbitrum DAO
items.desc: A U.S. attorney is attempting to claim ETH frozen by Arbitrum following the Kelp DAO exploit — on behalf of victims of North Korean state hackers from prior incidents.

items.title: Western Union Stablecoin USDPT Launches on Solana
items.desc: Western Union's USDPT stablecoin has gone live on the Solana blockchain, marking the traditional remittance giant's entry into on-chain digital assets.

items.title: Bitcoin Core 31.0 released
items.desc: Bitcoin Core version 31.0 is now available for download. See the release notes for more information about the bug fixes in this release. If you have any questions, please stop by the #bitcoin IRC chatroom (IRC, web) and we’ll do our best to help you.
